Use the Minimum Length
Use the minimum length of the two CharSequences instead of the maximum length to avoid the IndexOutOfBoundsException.

Check for Length Equality Before Calling regionMatches
Check if the lengths of the two CharSequences are equal before calling the regionMatches function. If they are not equal, return false immediately.

Use a Loop to Compare Characters
Instead of using the regionMatches function, use a loop to compare the characters of the two CharSequences one by one. This approach avoids the IndexOutOfBoundsException altogether.

Final Answer:

Root Cause: The root cause of the bug is in the line where the regionMatches function is called. The length of the region to match is set to the maximum length of the two CharSequences, which can cause an IndexOutOfBoundsException if the lengths are not equal.

Use the Minimum Length
Use the minimum length of the two CharSequences instead of the maximum length to avoid the IndexOutOfBoundsException.

Check for Length Equality Before Calling regionMatches
Check if the lengths of the two CharSequences are equal before calling the regionMatches function. If they are not equal, return false immediately.

Use a Loop to Compare Characters
Instead of using the regionMatches function, use a loop to compare the characters of the two CharSequences one by one. This approach avoids the IndexOutOfBoundsException altogether.
